checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or inventory-related concept present in the provided data set (company reports CostOfServices, consistent with a services business)
evidence.location: Series listing - absence of inventory concept

evidence.quote: GrossProfit 2013 annual=249719456.44 vs SalesRevenueServicesNet 2013 annual=453889907.19 (55.0% margin); GrossProfit 2014 annual=276330954.86 vs SalesRevenueServicesNet 2014 annual=510500608.32 (54.1% margin)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Quarterly income statements FY2014 Q4 through FY2015 Q3

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2011-06-30 value=245067.97; end=2011-09-30 value=245067.97; end=2011-12-31 value=245067.97; end=2012-03-31 value=245067.97; end=2012-06-30 value=245067.97; end=2012-09-30 value=245067.97

evidence.location: Same period balance sheets - AR nearly doubled while allowance held flat at exactly $245,067.97

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K or NT 10-Q forms appear in the filing chronology across 2011-2016

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available from 2008-12-31 through 2015-09-30 for Assets, Revenue, AR, Allowance, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow
No InventoryNet or SG&A-specific reserve line items provided

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables (and implicitly revenue) appear to have grown materially faster than sales in several periods (e.g., AR +31.6% in FY2014 vs revenue +12.5%; AR +15.4% Q1->Q2 2014 vs revenue +4.5%), while the allowance for doubtful accounts was held constant at exactly $245,067.97 for six consecutive quarterly reporting dates (2011-06-30 through 2012-09-30) even as AR nearly doubled from $50.5M to $79.8M. This pattern is consistent with under-provisioning for bad debts / delayed write-offs, which would understate the allowance and overstate net receivables and net income, and/or with aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., extended payment terms, bill-and-hold) inflating both revenue and AR.

Persistent multi-year gap between reported net income and operating cash flow (e.g., FY2013 NI=$11.9M vs OCF=$51.4M; FY2014 NI=$7.05M vs OCF=$47.0M) suggests large non-cash charges (D&A, stock-based compensation, deferred revenue/working-capital timing) are materially affecting reported earnings independent of cash generation, which could mask underlying volatility or reflect earnings-quality issues even though the reported NI series itself is volatile.

A sharp jump in Goodwill (from $155.8M at 2015-03-31 to $182.6M at 2015-06-30) and intangibles (from $65.0M to $87.5M over the same period) coincides with a swing to large operating and net losses in Q2/Q3 2015 (OperatingIncomeLoss -$4.75M and -$8.61M; NetIncomeLoss -$6.09M and -$10.8M), consistent with an acquisition whose integration costs, purchase accounting adjustments, or early impairment indicators may not be fully reflected, raising risk that goodwill/intangibles are currently overstated pending future impairment testing.

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew materially faster than revenue in FY2014 (AR +31.6% vs revenue +12.5%) and in Q2 2014 (AR +15.4% QoQ vs revenue +4.5%), indicating rising days-sales-outstanding and potential revenue/collectability quality issues

Allowance for doubtful accounts was held flat at exactly $245,067.97 across six consecutive quarterly filings (2011-06-30 through 2012-09-30) while accounts receivable nearly doubled from $50.5M to $79.8M, suggesting stale/under-provisioned bad debt reserves

Persistent multi-year divergence between net income and operating cash flow (e.g., FY2013 NI $11.9M vs OCF $51.4M; FY2014 NI $7.1M vs OCF $47.0M), warranting scrutiny of non-cash addbacks and earnings quality

Large goodwill/intangible asset jump (Goodwill $155.8M -> $182.6M, Q1-Q2 2015) coincident with a swing to significant operating and net losses in Q2/Q3 2015, raising acquisition-accounting and impairment risk
